References in TextSection 601(a)(2), referred to in par. (2), is title VI, , , which directed a duty rate of “Free” in the rate column numbered 1 of the Tariff Schedules of the United States for articles classified under specified items between 518.51 and 772.65 which the President determines would provide coverage comparable to that provided by foreign countries in the Annex to the Agreement on Trade in Civil Aircraft if such articles were certified for use in civil aircraft in accordance with headnote 3 to schedule 6, part 6, subpart C of the Tariff Schedules of the United States. The Tariff Schedules of the United States were replaced by the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States, which is not set out in the Code. See Publication of Harmonized Tariff Schedule note set out under section 1202 of the title.section 601(a)(2) of Pub. L. 96–3993 Stat. 267July 26, 1979

children.heading: Agreement
children.label: 1 Agreement
children.text: The term “Agreement” means the Agreement on Government Procurement referred to in , as submitted to the Congress, but including rectifications, modifications, and amendments which are accepted by the United States.section 3511(d)(17) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: all aircraft other than aircraft to be purchased for use by the Department of Defense or the United States Coast Guard;

children.label: B
children.text: the engines (and parts and components for incorporation therein) of such aircraft;

children.label: C
children.text: any other parts, components, and subassemblies for incorporation in such aircraft; and

children.label: D
children.text: any ground flight simulators, and parts and components thereof, for use with respect to such aircraft,

children.heading: Developed countries
children.label: 3 Developed countries
children.text: The term “developed countries” means countries so designated by the President.

children.label: i
children.text: a party to the Agreement, a product or service of that country or instrumentality which is covered under the Agreement for procurement by the United States;

children.label: ii
children.text: Mexico, as a party to the USMCA (as defined in ), a product or service of that country or instrumentality which is covered under the USMCA for procurement by the United States;section 4502 of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: a party to a free trade agreement that entered into force with respect to the United States after , and before , a product or service of that country or instrumentality which is covered under the free trade agreement for procurement by the United States;December 31, 2003January 2, 2005

children.label: iv
children.text: a party to the Dominican Republic-Central America-United States Free Trade Agreement, a product or service of that country or instrumentality which is covered under that Agreement for procurement by the United States;

children.label: v
children.text: a party to a free trade agreement that entered into force with respect to the United States after , and before , a product or service of that country or instrumentality which is covered under the free trade agreement for procurement by the United States;December 31, 2005July 2, 2006

children.label: vi
children.text: a party to the United States-Oman Free Trade Agreement, a product or service of that country or instrumentality which is covered under that Agreement for procurement by the United States;

children.label: vii
children.text: a party to the United States-Peru Trade Promotion Agreement, a product or service of that country or instrumentality which is covered under that agreement for procurement by the United States;

children.label: viii
children.text: a party to the United States–Korea Free Trade Agreement, a product or service of that country or instrumentality which is covered under that agreement for procurement by the United States;

children.label: ix
children.text: a party to the United States–Colombia Trade Promotion Agreement, a product or service of that country or instrumentality which is covered under that agreement for procurement by the United States; or

children.label: x
children.text: a party to the United States–Panama Trade Promotion Agreement, a product or service of that country or instrumentality which is covered under that agreement for procurement by the United States.

children.heading: Rule of origin
children.label: B Rule of origin
children.text: An article is a product of a country or instrumentality only if (i) it is wholly the growth, product, or manufacture of that country or instrumentality, or (ii) in the case of an article which consists in whole or in part of materials from another country or instrumentality, it has been substantially transformed into a new and different article of commerce with a name, character, or use distinct from that of the article or articles from which it was so transformed.

children.heading: Lowered threshold for certain products as a consequence of United States-Israel free trade area provisions
children.label: C Lowered threshold for certain products as a consequence of United States-Israel free trade area provisions
i
children.text: the Agreement on the Establishment of a Free Trade Area between the Government of the United States of America and the Government of Israel, regardless of the thresholds provided for in the Agreement (as defined in paragraph (1)), or

children.label: ii
children.text: any subsequent agreement between the United States and Israel which lowers on a reciprocal basis the applicable threshold for entities covered by the Agreement.

children.heading: Lowered threshold for certain products as a consequence of United States-Canada Free-Trade Agreement
children.label: D Lowered threshold for certain products as a consequence of United States-Canada Free-Trade Agreement
children.text: Except as otherwise agreed by the United States and Canada under paragraph 3 of article 1304 of the United States-Canada Free-Trade Agreement, the term “eligible product” includes a product or service of Canada having a contract value of $25,000 or more that would be covered for procurement by the United States under the Agreement (as defined in paragraph (1)), but for the thresholds provided for in the Agreement.

children.heading: Instrumentality
children.label: 5 Instrumentality
children.text: The term “instrumentality” shall not be construed to include an agency or division of the government of a country, but may be construed to include such arrangements as the European Economic Community.

children.heading: Least developed country
children.label: 6 Least developed country
children.text: The term “least developed country” means any country on the United Nations General Assembly list of least developed countries.

children.heading: Major industrial country
children.label: 7 Major industrial country
children.text: The term “major industrial country” means any such country as defined in and any instrumentality of such a country.section 2136 of this title
